heroHeadline: CJNG Crisis Day 61 — From Tenochtitlán (1325) to Sheinbaum's Mexico
heroSubtitle: Mexico's 701-year national arc spans the founding of México-Tenochtitlán (1325), the Spanish Conquest (1521), 300 years of colonial New Spain, the Independence War (1810–1821), the Reform era and French Intervention, the Porfiriato, the Mexican Revolution (1910–1920), seven decades of PRI hegemony, and the democratic transition of 2000. Today, President Claudia Sheinbaum — Mexico's first female president — governs amid the CJNG succession crisis (Day 61 since El Mencho's killing), record remittances of $64.7 billion, US-Mexico USMCA renegotiation pressure, and the FIFA World Cup opening in 48 days.
footerNote: Comprehensive national intelligence dashboard aggregating Mexico's full historical arc from 1325 to present. Data sourced from INEGI, Banxico, SESNSP, SEDENA, SRE, OHCHR, InSight Crime, and major international outlets.
